ddls:
CREATE TABLE "disciplines" (
  "discipline_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each discipline */,
  "discipline_name" TEXT /* Name of the engineering discipline */,
  "department_head" TEXT /* Name of the department head */,
  "department_email" TEXT /* Email address of the department head */,
  "department_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the department head */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("discipline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Full name of the student */,
  "discipline_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the discipline the student belongs to */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the student */,
  "cgpa" REAL /* Cumulative grade point average of the student */,
  "backlogs" INTEGER /* Number of backlogs ( failed subjects ) of the student */,
  "resume" TEXT /* Resume of the student (file path or URL) */,
  "linkedin_profile" TEXT /* LinkedIn profile URL of the student */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_discipline_id FOREIGN KEY ("discipline_id") REFERENCES disciplines ("discipline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_offers" (
  "offer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job offer record */,
  "discipline_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the discipline related to the job offers */,
  "num_offers" INTEGER /* Number of job offers received for this discipline */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Description of the job */,
  "salary" INTEGER /* Salary offered for the job */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the job */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("offer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_offers_discipline_id FOREIGN KEY ("discipline_id") REFERENCES disciplines ("discipline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "double_offers" (
  "double_offer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each double offer record */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student who received double job offers */,
  "num_double_offers" INTEGER /* Number of double job offers received */,
  "offer1_id" INTEGER /* ID of the first job offer */,
  "offer2_id" INTEGER /* ID of the second job offer */,
  "decision" TEXT /* Decision of the student (e.g., accepted, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("double_offer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_double_offers_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_double_offers_offer2_id FOREIGN KEY ("offer2_id") REFERENCES job_offers ("offer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_double_offers_offer1_id FOREIGN KEY ("offer1_id") REFERENCES job_offers ("offer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "placements" (
  "placement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each placement record */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student placed */,
  "placement_company" TEXT /* Name of the company where the student got placed */,
  "placement_date" TEXT /* Date of placement */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "salary" INTEGER /* Salary offered for the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("placement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_placements_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "placement_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistical record */,
  "discipline_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the discipline for which statistics are maintained */,
  "eligible_students" INTEGER /* Number of students eligible for placements */,
  "placed_students" INTEGER /* Number of students who have been placed */,
  "placement_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of students placed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_placement_statistics_discipline_id FOREIGN KEY ("discipline_id") REFERENCES disciplines ("discipline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recruiters" (
  "recruiter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recruiter */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the recruiting company */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the recruiter */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the recruiter */,
  "company_website" TEXT /* Website of the recruiting company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the recruiting company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recruiter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recruitment_drives" (
  "drive_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recruitment drive */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recruiting company conducting the drive */,
  "drive_date" TEXT /* Date of the recruitment drive */,
  "venue" TEXT /* Venue of the recruitment drive */,
  "drive_type" TEXT /* Type of the recruitment drive (e.g., on-campus, off-campus) */,
  "drive_status" TEXT /* Status of the recruitment drive (e.g., scheduled, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("drive_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recruitment_drives_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES recruiters ("recruiter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student applying for the job */,
  "offer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job offer being applied for */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date of application submission */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the application (e.g., submitted, interview, accepted) */,
  "resume" TEXT /* Resume of the student (file path or URL) */,
  "cover_letter" TEXT /* Cover letter of the student (file path or URL) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_offer_id FOREIGN KEY ("offer_id") REFERENCES job_offers ("offer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role (e.g., student, recruiter, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
